matches.rationale: Same Bash tool and same pre-pull-request library-check target; the extracted rule explicitly includes make format. It also bundles make lint and make test, but 1:1 matching permits assigning it only once.
Same Edit tool and same target: edits introducing Sphinx-style double-backtick inline code in source-file docstrings or comments.

misses.note: The compiler bundled make lint, make format, and make test into the single run-checks-before-pr rule. That rule is already assigned to the closest first listed command (g1) and cannot be reused under the 1:1 constraint.
The compiler bundled make test, make format, and make lint into the single run-checks-before-pr rule. That rule is already assigned to g1 and cannot be reused under the 1:1 constraint.
Compiler-self-reported skip: "Usage note that the TEST variable can be passed to `make test` to run a specific file or extra pytest options — a usage catalog with no attached obligation/prohibition".

The positive single-backtick instruction was bundled into no-double-backtick-edit with the double-backtick prohibition. That extracted rule is more directly targeted to g5 by its name and pattern and cannot also match g6 under the 1:1 constraint.

unmatched_extracted.note: This is a real action-bindable Write rule implementing the document's prohibition on double-backtick inline code, but the frozen gold ruleset lists only the Edit-tool counterpart.
